attribution: Transcript data: TalkMoves dataset (SumnerLab / Suresh et al.), CC BY-NC-SA 4.0, speaker-blinded before any model call.
caveats: The calculator checks a claim only when it can ground it; 'not checked' means outside its current coverage, not wrong.
Reading a speaker's move is interpretive. The model proposes the reading; only the claim verdicts are computed.
One automated read of one transcript — a starting point for your own reading, not a verdict on the discussion.

transcript:
# tm_0079

U0001 S01: I would like someone to tell me where you would place one.
U0002 S01: You can talk to  your partner if you want too but, I would like you all to think about where you  would place one.
U0003 S01: On the one Alan made.
U0004 S01: On Alan's line.
U0005 S01: Some of you think we need to make the number line longer.
U0006 S01: Some of you do not  think we need to make the number line longer.
U0007 S01: Okay.
U0008 S01: Also, what reasons you have for why your think so or why not.
U0009 S01: By the way … if you have worked that one out, I am curious where you would  put four-fourths.
U0010 S02: [T walks over to Gregory and Danielle] You have a very interesting concept.
U0011 S02: Why don't you share that—go ahead.
U0012 S01: Why don't you go talk to Alan or Meredith?
U0013 S01: See what they  have to say …  [Students walk over to Meredith.]
U0014 S01: Where would you put four-fourths Erik and Michael?
U0015 S01: Where would you put five- fifths?
U0016 S01: Seven-sevenths?
U0017 S01: A million-millionths?
U0018 S01: Are you prepared to argue that  position?
U0019 S01: Michael, why?
U0020 S03: Because ….
U0021 S01: Okay, so you are telling me that that is just another name for one?
U0022 S01: You want to  put those number on the bottom.
U0023 S01: Remember we agreed you must put the  numbers on the bottom.
U0024 S01: Erik so forcefully told us that people will be confused.
U0025 S01: Alan had a very nice method.
U0026 S01: Ok.  Are we ready to come back and share our ideas?
U0027 S01: I would like all of us to listen to some.
U0028 S01: We have at least two different  positions, here.
U0029 S01: I think I will give Meredith the first crack at an argument  because it was her number line that raised the question to me.
U0030 S01: And, this is  something I am, of course, going to want you to write about—about your  arguments and why you think so to see if we can come up with some common  understanding of where to place some of these numbers.
U0031 S01: Are you ready?
U0032 S01: Ok.  Meredith, go ahead.
U0033 S04: You asked me where to place one.
U0034 S04: I think if you have thirds—one third, two  thirds, three thirds—three thirds would be equal to one.
U0035 S04: See because one third,  two thirds,  and three thirds   —three thirds is the same as saying one.
U0036 S04: Four fourths is the same as saying one.
U0037 S04: A hundred hundredths is the same thing as saying one.
U0038 S01: What do you think?
U0039 S01: Michael?
U0040 S03: I think that if you have a number with the same number on top as in the bottom,  then it is always going to be equal to the number named one.
U0041 S01: Who disagree with that?
U0042 S01: Who disagrees with that idea?
U0043 S01: Who has a question about that idea?
U0044 S01: Jessica you are making a face, are you confused?
U0045 S05: I think, yeah, I agree with her.
U0046 S01: Alan, do you have a comment?
U0047 S06: No, what I was saying before when I was talking is that zero to the  one third  mark is one third.
U0048 S06: zero to the two thirds mark is two thirds.
U0049 S06: Zero to the three  thirds mark is three thirds.
U0050 S06: Now, three thirds you cannot have any more thirds or  you would have four thirds.
U0051 S06: Then you have to make the thirds bigger or not have  another.
U0052 S06: You can only have three thirds.
U0053 S01: Okay.
U0054 S01: Let us hold that question about what would happen if we had  four thirds.
U0055 S04: You only have four thirds, if you are going to have that you could only have four  fourths not four thirds, you can have four thirds.
U0056 S01: You cannot have four thirds not in that interval.
U0057 S01: I wonder if we could have four  thirds if we went further?
U0058 S04: Then we would have to have six thirds.
U0059 S01: Then, we would have to have six thirds.
U0060 S01: Ok.
U0061 S01: Hold on.
U0062 S01: Meredith just said—I  want to make sure you are all able to hear what she just said—I believe she said  that you cannot have four thirds in this interval, but I asked the question … if you  extend the interval, could have four thirds?
U0063 S04: If you made it to two, you would have six thirds, and then you could place four  thirds.
U0064 S01: If you made it to two, you would have six thirds, and then you could place four  thirds.
U0065 S01: That is a very interesting idea.
U0066 S01: Bryan, what did you have to say?
U0067 S07: Alan thinks that I am placing four thirds and I am not.
U0068 S07: Because you see, I think  that you would start on the negative side.
U0069 S07: Like umm … [stands up and walks to  overhead]  For example, you would probably start on the one third and right  between the one third   and the two thirds would be one third, right   between the two thirds and the three thirds   would be two thirds and, you see, he thinks that when I add that there, he thinks  that zero to one third is a fraction, it is not.
U0070 S07: Because zero is a separate number  and it is not a fraction, and so I would think that if you start on the one third and  anything pass that if you keep going up you would hit two thirds and anything  between there would be one third.
U0071 S07: But if you start at zero and you go across and  you hit the one third, zero is not a fraction, so if you add one more, zero between  one third is not a fraction, so it would be four thirds.
U0072 S01: Some people have written zero as zero thirds.
U0073 S06: Okay.
U0074 S06: What I was saying before is this …
U0075 S01: Let us see what Meredith is doing here.
U0076 S01: Well, that is very   interesting.
U0077 S01: Very interesting what Meredith is doing up there.
U0078 S06: Okay.
U0079 S06: What I am saying is that zero to the one third mark—anything from zero to  one third—is the one third.
U0080 S06: Now, anything pass the one third mark we would be  calling one third, zero.
U0081 S07: See, one third is higher than zero …
U0082 S06: Right.
U0083 S07: … So, you would not count zero to one third as one third.
U0084 S01: Okay.
U0085 S01: Maybe let us stop for a moment.
U0086 S01: I know this is very interesting and I  know we want to continue this.
U0087 S01: You want to say something else, Alan?
U0088 S06: What I am saying is.
U0089 S06: Here is a model of using thirds.
U0090 S06: Suppose that end is zero and that end is one.
U0091 S06: You are saying from there to there—this piece—really has no fraction value in  the one third.
U0092 S07: There is no fraction value.
U0093 S06: You cannot put that over there, look, is has extra room.
U0094 S06: You only have three  spaces—one, two, three—you have one third from zero to one third, two thirds  from one third to two thirds, and three thirds area from two thirds to three thirds.
U0095 S01: Okay.
U0096 S01: Let us just hear from Meredith and then I want to do something else.
U0097 S01: We  have lot of good ideas that we need to think about.
U0098 S01: Meredith, what is your  comment?
U0099 S04: Well, I put one third, two thirds, and then three thirds would equal one.
U0100 S04: And,  then, I went from zero to two it would be one and one third , two and   two thirds , and, then, one and three thirds.
U0101 S01: One and two thirds, right?
U0102 S07: There is no fraction value between zero and one third, because…
U0103 S06: If you have no value between zero and one third, then look, you   eliminate that how many spaces would you have?
U0104 S06: Two.
U0105 S07: What I would think would be that if you start on the lower number  and then keep going up and until you hit that.
U0106 S07: Anything between there would be the one third and the two thirds right   there.
U0107 S07: And, if you keep going up to there you will hit that and,   that will be two thirds   and if you put a bar right there it would count as one,   two thirds between 1 would be three thirds.
U0108 S07: Because, like I was saying, if you started there at zero,    then zero does not have value …
U0109 S01: Okay.
U0110 S01: We need to sit down.
U0111 S01: Obviously, we have not convinced Bryan.
U0112 S01: Alan and Meredith have tried and not convinced Bryan.
U0113 S01: I wanted to give Sarah and Beth  and Audra a chance to share their idea and if everyone listen a bit more, we  will  see what we are thinking in a few minutes.
U0114 S08: We thought that we did not have to put anything else on the number line, because  if we put this from zero to 1 and you would mark one third here because if you  used a ruler here to measure it or something  one third would go here,  two thirds would go here,  three thirds would go here,because the length will be the same as Cuisenaire rods  or something.
U0115 S01: What I am not clear about when I am looking up there, I am not sure, you wrote a  number on top, and I thought we all agreed that we would write our number line  with the numbers in the bottom.
U0116 S01: Where would you place the three-thirds?
U0117 S08: We would place it here
U0118 S01: Could you write it underneath the number line and tell us why you would place it  there.
U0119 S01: Because, I think some of us are interested in where you would place it.
U0120 S01: [Groups workstogether on placing the three thirds on the bottom of the line.]
U0121 S01: And, you are telling me you would place it with 1 to the right of three-thirds?
U0122 S01: [No verbal response from group.]
U0123 S01: So this clearly defines where we have  differences of opinions, right?
U0124 S01: Is that true?
U0125 S01: So, how many of you are agreeing  that we should place 1 to the right of three thirds?
U0126 S01: How many of you believe  that?
U0127 S01: Oh.
U0128 S01: How many of you believe that it should go to the left of three-thirds?
U0129 S01: How many of you believe it should go right on top of the one?
U0130 S08: That's what we meant.
U0131 S08: We just could not get it right on top to fit.
U0132 S01: You meant to put it on top?
U0133 S09: You just put it under it.
U0134 S01: Oh, I see, you just could not fit it in.
U0135 S01: So, Jessica, how could they do it to put it by  the one?
U0136 S01: You want to go show them how to do it?
U0137 S01: They meant the same thing, Okay.
U0138 S01: So,  I am hearing that we have some agreement here then; you all agree that three  thirds would go under one.
U0139 S01: It would go in the same spot.
U0140 S01: How many agree with  that?
U0141 S01: [Six students on camera raise their hands]  And, I would like to hear again  why that would work, could you tell me Erin?
U0142 S01: Why you would put it there and  not to the right or to the left?
U0143 S01: Any idea?
U0144 S01: Want to think about it?
U0145 S01: J.
U0146 S10: I want to think about it a little more.
U0147 S01: Okay.
U0148 S01: James?
U0149 S11: I think one half and one half makes a whole and four—fourths would make a  whole and three thirds would make a whole also, so it will be right on top of the  one.
U0150 S01: Okay.
U0151 S01: Are you agreeing with that Jakki?
U0152 S12: Yes.
U0153 S01: Some of you are nodding your heads.
U0154 S01: Okay.
U0155 S01: So, I am hearing some agreement  on that and that is where you would place it
